name: Frohn et al. Synthetic Pre-pilot Scoring Agreement Framework Extract
population_or_context: Constructed-response item development and live-scoring validation workflow for commercial LLM scoring systems.
sample_size: Framework guidance rather than one empirical dataset; the paper recommends power analysis and often hundreds of synthetic responses per score level.
methods: Extracted the paper's pre-pilot validation guidance for labeled synthetic student responses, human labels, scorer-agreement thresholds, challenge sets, and pilot validation on real student responses.
education_context: Constructed-response assessment items with checklist, analytic, or holistic rubrics in live assessment contexts.
response_origin: Synthetic student responses are proposed as proxies for authentic work during pre-pilot validation and challenge-set development; real student responses remain required for pilot validation.
rubric_label_space: Constructed-response labels such as correct, partially correct, incorrect, checklist criteria, or rubric score points.
label_source: The framework calls for accurate human labels on synthetic responses when evaluating LLM scorer performance and human scoring of real pilot responses for authentic validation.
generation_model: Commercial LLM scoring framework; the synthetic response generator is not pinned to one model version.
generation_method: Generate balanced synthetic response datasets and challenge sets covering score levels, plausible response diversity, and LLM-specific failure modes.
prompt_strategy: Item-specific scoring prompts include question, criteria, and response; synthetic response generation prompts are framework-level rather than reported as a fixed empirical protocol.
evaluation_method: Evaluate LLM scoring against human labels with agreement, accuracy, F1, Matthews correlation coefficient, false-positive/false-negative rates, score distributions, and conditional accuracy.
scorer_use: Pre-pilot validation, calibration, item revision, challenge testing, and operational monitoring of LLM-based constructed-response scorers.
real_response_comparator: Real student pilot responses are required after synthetic pre-pilot validation to establish scoring accuracy under authentic conditions.
reported_metrics: The paper discusses Cohen kappa, quadratic weighted kappa, accuracy, F1, Matthews correlation coefficient, false-positive and false-negative rates, score-distribution comparisons, and agreement with human labels.
limitations: This is an implementation framework, not an empirical demonstration on one synthetic dataset.
Synthetic responses are treated as pre-pilot evidence, not a replacement for real student response validation.
The guidance is bounded to constructed-response LLM scoring and requires concrete item/rubric implementation before strong claims can be made.
